---
title: "Morning Track"
---

## Goal

Give every attendee enough confidence to create, ground, and publish an agent in Copilot Studio.

:::: {.lesson-grid}
::: {.lesson-panel}
### 1. [Start fast and reduce friction](sign-in-and-tour.qmd)

Get everyone logged in and oriented without turning the opening into a lecture.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 2. [Instructions create the first win](first-agent.qmd)

Show how role, goals, and boundaries shape the first agent.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 3. [Good files beat clever prompts](file-grounding.qmd)

Use documents and spreadsheets to show visible grounding value.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 4. [Web knowledge scales the pattern](web-knowledge.qmd)

Turn a public page into a knowledge source and test it live.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 5. [MCP explains how agents do more](mcp-overview.qmd)

Introduce the concept without drowning the room in protocol details.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### 6. [Publishing turns a demo into a product](publishing-channels.qmd)

Explain where agents go after the build phase.
:::
::::

## Attendee Options

:::: {.choice-grid}
::: {.choice-card}
### Option A

Work with a document about Swedish electricity zones.
:::

::: {.choice-card}
### Option B

Work with a spreadsheet on installed solar capacity.
:::

::: {.choice-card}
### Option C

Work with a pricing dataset over time.
:::
::::

## Outputs

By lunch, each attendee should have:

- one working agent
- one grounded data source
- one web-based knowledge configuration
- one publishing target they understand

::: {.callout-tip}
## Teaching rhythm

Keep the morning hands-on, forgiving, and fast. The purpose is momentum.
:::
